Delivered reactive seed-search tooling and default-on peel/dense fixup path. Current validated result: [2048,32000] fuzz 0 failures, mean overhead near documented floor with known rare-tail/high-N residuals split to wirehair-0yk; long-term full table regeneration remains wirehair-jwg.

Delivered joint peel+dense correction-table support and root fixup tables. Packaged 605 peel correction rows and 91 dense correction rows; clean CMake and harness builds; focused fuzz 52,064 trials with 0 correctness failures. Remaining high-N reactive work is tracked by wirehair-0yk.

Explored and implemented the remaining per-block fast path. Full-output recovery already used CAT_COPY_FIRST_N to copy received systematic blocks; ReconstructBlock now also directly copies a received original block from input staging instead of recomputing it. Verified with focused recover_block API test, CMake build, harness build, and 35,712-trial fuzz with 0 failures.

Prerequisite residual-component measurement completed under WH_COUNT. Deferred-column graph is already almost fully decomposed (e.g. N=2048: 46 cols/46 components; N=8192: 75/75; N=16000: 123/123; repair-only N=8192: 89 cols/87 comps max=3). Nested-dissection/Schur rewrite is not justified: sparse deferred part is already split and remaining coupling is dense/mix separator; GE/back-sub traffic is small.
Implemented AVX2/AVX-512 gf256_memswap widening and added memswap micro selfcheck/throughput coverage.
Added top-byte starting lookup for GeneratePeelRowWeight tail scan; verified exact equivalence against old distribution.
Kept the y<=1 special-case as one unlikely branch so the common multiply path avoids an extra branch; measured with micro and end-to-end bench.
Removed variable modulo from IterateNextColumn, added small-prime shortcut, and verified helper equivalence against old formulas.
Added scoped likely/unlikely hints to hot codec rare/common branches; default end-to-end mostly neutral to positive, largest gain at N=8192 in normal build.
Measured -funroll-loops/-fno-semantic-interposition variants; not kept as defaults because encode/recover were mixed to negative.
Implemented WH_ALIGN64 opt-in for 64-byte workspace bases and GE row pitch; measured mixed results, so left it gated rather than default.
Implemented/measured in optimization experiment pass; kept useful knobs gated where results were mixed.

All small-wins child experiments are implemented/measured and closed; remaining work belongs to larger algorithm/offline-search issues.
Implemented safe identity-multiplier fast path and measured neutral/mixed impact.
Added sweep support and measured 4/5-row variants invalid without regeneration; default unchanged.

Removed experimental peeling code from mainline codec, added experiments/peeling area marker, and completed repeated validation passes.
Standalone peeling harness, results, literature cross-check, and repeated validation passes completed.
Completed expanded standalone peeling heuristic/statistic sweep and documented final results.

Implemented deterministic per-trial N jitter around anchor sizes, added sampled dimension and overhead CSV columns, self-tests for range/determinism/paired matrices, all-anchor jitter smoke, repeated bug-read passes, normal and sanitizer self-tests.
Implemented incremental live-ref metric cache in standalone peeling harness with local avalanche updates; validated against exact scans in self-test, sanitizer self-test, and 32k smoke.
Implemented incremental degree-2 metric cache in standalone peeling harness with local avalanche updates; validated against exact scans in self-test, sanitizer self-test, and 32k smoke.
Implemented incremental fill metric cache in standalone peeling harness with local avalanche updates; validated against exact scans in self-test, sanitizer self-test, and 32k smoke.
Added self-test that compares lazy cached live-ref/fill/degree-2 selections against exact selectors on deterministic generated matrices.
Added lazy equivalence walk that mutates the graph through inactivation avalanche and checks cached metrics after each step; normal and sanitizer self-tests pass.

Implemented incremental lazy metric cache variants and tests; all lazy children closed after normal self-test, sanitizer self-test, targeted all-anchor jitter smoke, 32k lazy smoke, and repeated bug-read passes.

Fresh sweep complete and validated
Row-first inactivation methods are implemented in the standalone peeling harness and covered by full-method self-tests/sweep
Added and validated high-cap LT variants with focused results
Audit completed; caveats documented and no implementation bug found
Dense binary smoke baseline added and reported

Completed N=320 recipe ablation sweep: added N=320-focused structures, ran all-method zero-overhead and +1/+2/+4/+8 overhead sweeps over 4x100 trials, validated with harness self-tests, ASan/UBSan self-test/smoke, CSV integrity checks, and documented results in experiments/peeling/results/SUMMARY.md.
N=3200 recipe ablation sweep completed, validated, summarized, and recorded.
XOR-cost metrics implemented, validated, frontier rerun completed, and results summarized.

Reduced frontier sweep completed and summarized. Ran selected 25 structures x 15 methods at N=1000,6400,32000 with N-jitter=10, fixed overheads 0/1/2, paired matrix seeds, and 400 trials per aggregate row. Validation passed for shard integrity, aggregate integrity, build, self-test, and diff whitespace.

Implemented XOR block calibration, cheaper boundary approximations, focused 100-trial paired sweeps at N=1000/6400/32000 with overhead 0/1/2, saved aggregate results, and validated with build, self-test, sanitizer self-test, CSV checks, and diff check.

Measured 100 KiB block XOR cost, added it to the calibration results, regenerated the focused sweep aggregate with 100 KiB XOR-equivalent cost columns, updated the summary, and validated with build, self-test, smoke benchmark, CSV checks, and diff check.
Prototype, validation, and small/medium/large retest completed; local avalanche lookahead is documented as not cost-effective enough to ship in this form.
Implemented isolated codec/ v2 peel policy scaffold with PeelingCodec selector and focused policy test
Implemented modulo-N SeedProfile selector with table seed lookup, exact-N peel/dense fixup detection, deterministic candidate seed generation, focused unit coverage, and repeated bug-check passes.

Implemented H=0 peel row generator/evaluator with residual column and unique residual-row accounting plus matrix/dense/total XOR estimates; validated by unit tests, diff checks, and repeated code-reading bug passes.
Added densecheck benchmark mode for real encode/decode dense-seed candidate trials; ran small-block known-fixup and 1 MiB-block smoke checks with zero failures and repeated code-reading bug passes.

Implemented isolated wirehair_v2::Codec encoder/decoder facade that selects/injects SeedProfile seeds into the underlying solver; unit-tested real encode/decode/recover including random loss and final partial block recovery after repeated bug passes.
Added wirehair_v2_bench compare mode for random-N random-loss baseline-vs-v2 overhead and throughput reporting; fixed loss-stream fairness during bug pass and validated with deterministic smoke runs.

Ran final focused build/test, diff check, densecheck, and random-N compare experiments from the final binary; zero decode failures in all smoke comparisons, and repeated bug-fix passes found/fixed residual-row double counting, benchmark loss-stream mismatch, memory-cap sampling, and stale options.

Implemented the v2 seed-tree selector, H=0 residual/XOR evaluator, dense seed check harness, isolated codec wrapper, and random-N baseline comparison benchmark under codec/ with focused tests and repeated bug-check passes.

Implemented shared BuildPeelSolvePlan row-generation/evaluation API under codec/, wired seed tuning through it, added deterministic unit coverage for plan seeds, H=0/H>0 row counts, and tuned metadata; repeated code-reading bug passes found and fixed stale fixup flags.

Broadened compare mode with overhead variance/percentiles and ran random N=[64,6400], 8 trials per block-size for 1280,102400,1048576 byte blocks at 10% random loss with max_message_mib=256; baseline and v2 both decoded all smoke trials.

Added seedtable benchmark mode and ran N=320,1000,3200,6400,12000,32000 across 1280,102400,1048576 byte blocks with 16 peel candidates x 3 trials; output records tuned peel seed, residual mean/max, xor cost, and fixup flags.

Added densetune mode for real encode/decode dense seed candidates. Ran 1280/102400 byte sweeps for N=320,1000,3200 with 8 candidates x 2 trials and 1048576 byte sweep for N=96,160,320,1000 with 4 candidates x 2 trials. Repeated bug passes fixed all-fail mean reporting.

Ran 1000 paired baseline/v2 random-loss experiments for each block-byte parameter set: 1280, 102400, and 1048576 bytes, each with independent seed streams. All cohorts completed with no decode failures; results reported extra-symbol distribution and approximate per-message processing time.

Added a tuned-profile compare option and ran fixed-N baseline vs v2_tuned sweeps. Found the differentiator: base v2 is seed-equivalent to baseline, while tuned v2 changes overhead distributions. Tuned v2 improved some bands (e.g. N=12000/bb=1280: 0.030 -> 0.010 mean OH; capped 1MiB: 0.050 -> 0.000) but worsened others (e.g. N=12000/capped 100KiB: 0.020 -> 0.060; N=320/bb=1280: 0.014 -> 0.026). Throughput remained mostly within noise or slightly worse for tuned v2 on large payloads.

Implemented and tested two optimization-loop tools in codec/: compare --v2-profile auto with a conservative auto-min-delta threshold to avoid rare-event overfitting, and densecount to sweep dense row count deltas with paired seeds. Experiments found no universal peel/tuned-seed win; tuned seed calibration overfits unless thresholded. Dense count is a real tradeoff knob: reducing dense rows can improve create/decode throughput (e.g. N=320/100KiB dense -16 and N=12000/1280 dense -8), but candidates were loss-regime dependent and did not produce a universally safe overhead win. N=3200 dense -16 failed, establishing a safety bound.

Added densegrid paired dense-count/dense-seed sweep mode and compare-mode dense overrides. Repeated code-reading and smoke validation found no robust dense-count/seed candidate suitable for default policy: densegrid produced local candidates, but direct compare validation under the baseline-v2 random-loss protocol regressed or only traded overhead for slower decode. Production seed policy left unchanged.

Hunt executed via two multi-agent workflow runs (92+94 agents): 12 regions, 40+ candidates, 2-skeptic adversarial verification. 30+ confirmed findings fixed across three commits (production API/codec, gf256, v2 bench, whx/tiling harnesses); high-severity: 4GiB offset wrap (mitigated, full fix wirehair-hph), uint16 block-count truncation, OOM null deref. Remaining: codec-ge region + ~20 unverified candidates -> wirehair-qn0.

Completed adversarial bug hunt. All listed leads were either fixed or refuted in notes; final codec-GE review confirmed and fixed the scalar heavy back-substitution guard, and found no further pivot-map, heavy-row, windowing, or dense-value bugs.

Validated and adopted codec hardening fixes: blockBytes >= 2^31 is rejected before gf256 signed-int byte counts can truncate, AllocateMatrix clears only allocated GE rows under WH_ALIGN64, duplicate all-original decode failures no longer resume without GE state, and policy coverage now checks duplicate-original failure/reuse plus oversized blockBytes on both encoder and decoder creation. Validation: cmake --build build --target wirehair wirehair_v2_policy_test unit_test; bash bench/build.sh; ./build/codec/wirehair_v2_policy_test; ./bench/whx fuzz --threads 1 --secs 5 --nmax 1024 --seed 0xC0DEFACE; ./bench/whx ohead --threads 1 --trials 20 --bb 64 --startmode 1 --loss 0.10 --nlo 64 --nhi 256 --nstep 64; temporary WH_ALIGN64 ASan-only policy binary; git diff --check.

Added focused v2 policy coverage for huge-message decoder rejection before uint32 narrowing and for preserving the last good SeedProfile after failed decoder and encoder initialization overrides. Validation: cmake --build build --target wirehair_v2_policy_test; ./build/codec/wirehair_v2_policy_test; git diff --check.

Retired the misleading direct compare --v2-profile tuned mode so synthetic TuneSeedProfile output can no longer be injected into production p_seed trials without real validation. compare now accepts only base or auto, rejects tuned with an explicit model-transfer message, rejects unknown profile names, and docs point to auto for real-trial A/B validation or seedtable for offline diagnostics. Validation: cmake --build build --target wirehair_v2_bench wirehair_v2_policy_test; ./build/codec/wirehair_v2_policy_test; tuned profile rejection smoke; unknown profile rejection smoke; auto profile smoke; git diff --check.

Replaced scalar gf256 uint64_t/uint32_t dereferences in mobile non-NEON bulk loops and all 8/4-byte tails with memcpy-based unaligned load/store helpers, leaving x86 SIMD loadu/storeu paths untouched. Also annotated intentional byte-tail switch fallthroughs so rebuilding gf256.cpp is warning-clean. Validation: cmake --build build --target wirehair wirehair_v2_policy_test; ./build/codec/wirehair_v2_policy_test; bash bench/build.sh; ./bench/whx micro --threads 1 --secs 1; ./bench/whx fuzz --threads 1 --secs 3 --nmax 512 --seed 0xA11A6E; temporary -DGF256_TARGET_MOBILE -fsanitize=alignment,undefined harness over misaligned gf256 add/add2/addset/mul/muladd/memswap calls; git diff --check; rg confirmed no remaining uint64_t*/uint32_t* scalar casts in gf256.cpp/h.
Closed as no-go after feasibility evaluation: GPU offload targets the wrong bottleneck; prioritize symbol-XOR reduction/locality tasks instead.
Closed as completed negative experiment: cache-backed avalanche lookahead is exact but slower, so do not promote.
